rxn_class_summary: Electroinitiated cation chain reaction forming intramolecular C-C bond between thioacetal and pendant olefin to give tetrahydropyrans bearing an ArS group
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of ArSSAr generates ArS(ArSSAr)+ (an 'ArS+' equivalent) that activates the thioacetal; only a catalytic amount of electricity is required because ArS+ is regenerated in the chain

key_intermediates.name: Alkoxycarbenium ion 9 from thioacetal 6 after ArS+ abstraction of ArS
Cyclized carbocation 10 (tetrahydropyranyl cation after intramolecular olefin addition)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSSAr to an 'ArS+' equivalent [ArS(ArSSAr)+]; ArS+ abstracts ArS from thioacetal 6 to give alkoxycarbenium 9, which cyclizes onto the pendant olefin to give cation 10; ArSSAr traps 10 to deliver product 8 and regenerate ArS+, propagating the chain.
rate_determining_step: Intramolecular addition of the alkoxycarbenium ion to the olefin (cyclization of 9 to 10), made feasible by intramolecularization; the trapping of 10 by ArSSAr is concerted-like and accelerated by excess ArSSAr

probe_reagents.name: Bu4NBF4 supporting electrolyte (fluoride source probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Fluoride from BF4- attacks cyclized cation giving fluorinated product 7a (81%), proving a free cation intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Bu4NB(C6F5)4 (non-nucleophilic electrolyte)
probe_reagents.observation: Suppresses fluoride attack; ArSSAr instead traps the cyclized cation giving 8a (72-82%), confirming ArSSAr as nucleophile in the chain

probe_reagents.name: Substoichiometric ArS(ArSSAr)+ (20 mol%, 2 mol%)
probe_reagents.observation: Catalytic loading still produces 8a (33-47%), consistent with a chain mechanism; excess ArSSAr boosts yield to 82%

probe_reagents.name: CSI-MS / 1H NMR of ArS(ArSSAr)+B(C6F5)4-
probe_reagents.observation: Direct spectroscopic detection (HRMS m/z 381.0084) of the cationic ArS+ carrier

notes: Exclusive cis-selectivity in 8 (except 8b) suggests that cyclization of 9 to 10 and trapping by ArSSAr occur in a somewhat concerted manner rather than via a fully free, equilibrated cation 10. In-cell (direct) electrolysis (method B) also initiates the chain effectively. The reaction of alkoxycarbenium with a non-stabilized olefin is proposed as the potential bottleneck, rescued by intramolecularity.
